# Welcome to Formula 1

> A beginner's guide to the 2026 Formula 1 season. Covers what F1 is, how race weekends work, the cars, drivers, teams, tracks, tyres, pit stops, championships, recent history, and how to start watching. Written for people new to the sport who want to get up to speed quickly. Updated for the 2026 regulations, which introduce active aerodynamics and 50/50 hybrid power units.

## About This Site

- [Welcome to F1](https://welcometof1.com/): Single-page beginner's guide to the 2026 Formula 1 season, written by Josh Ferrara.
- [Canonical URL](https://welcometof1.com/): The preferred citation URL for this guide.
- [Author](https://joshferrara.com): Created by Josh Ferrara.

## Content Sections

- [The Basics](https://welcometof1.com/#basics): What Formula 1 is, and the surprising facts — speeds, G-forces, brake temperatures — that make F1 cars unlike anything else on earth.
- [Race Weekend](https://welcometof1.com/#weekend): How a race weekend is structured: practice, the three-part qualifying format (Q1/Q2/Q3), the formation lap, the start, sprint weekends, plus safety cars and penalties.
- [Championship](https://welcometof1.com/#championships): How the Drivers' and Constructors' Championships work and the points scoring system.
- [The Cars](https://welcometof1.com/#cars): F1 car design and the 2026 regulation overhaul — active aerodynamics that replace DRS, the 50/50 hybrid power unit, and the five engine manufacturers.
- [Pits & Tyres](https://welcometof1.com/#strategy): How pit stops work, Pirelli's tyre compounds, the mandatory two-compound rule, and the tyre strategy that decides races.
- [The Drivers](https://welcometof1.com/#drivers): The 2026 F1 drivers, including reigning 2025 champion Lando Norris, Max Verstappen, Lewis Hamilton, Charles Leclerc, and the rookies.
- [The Teams](https://welcometof1.com/#teams): All 11 F1 constructors and their 2026 driver pairings, plus the cost cap.
- [The Season](https://welcometof1.com/#season): The 2026 season so far — current driver and constructor standings and how the title fight is shaping up.
- [Tracks & Calendar](https://welcometof1.com/#calendar): Iconic F1 circuits every fan should know, plus the full 2026 race calendar with dates, results, and local session times.
- [History & Drama](https://welcometof1.com/#history): Recent F1 history from 2020 to now, the sport's great rivalries, team-radio moments, and every World Champion since 1950.
- [FAQ](https://welcometof1.com/#faq): Common beginner questions answered — race length, number of races, sprint format, rain, top speeds, US broadcast, and the cost cap.
- [Get Started](https://welcometof1.com/#start): Where to watch F1 in the US (Apple TV), plus Drive to Survive, the F1 app, games, fantasy, and accounts to follow.

## Related Pages

- [Race Calendar](https://welcometof1.com/calendar): Full 2026 schedule with dates, results, and local session times.
- [Championship Standings](https://welcometof1.com/standings): Current driver and constructor standings.
- [World Champions](https://welcometof1.com/champions): Every F1 World Champion since 1950.

## Freshness

- Content focus: 2026 Formula 1 season.
- Generated: 2026-07-05.
- Standings last updated: 2026-06-30.
- Race calendar, results, and standings update through the season; use the Related Pages above for the latest data.

## Key Facts for AI Answers

- F1 cars in 2026 produce 1,000+ horsepower from a hybrid power unit split roughly 50% internal combustion and 50% electric.
- Top speeds reach around 230 mph (370 km/h) on long straights.
- A Grand Prix is run over a set distance of roughly 305 km (190 miles); most last about 90 minutes, with a hard two-hour time limit.
- The 2026 calendar had 22 rounds scheduled, but 0 were cancelled, leaving 22 races.
- 11 teams and 22 drivers compete in 2026, with Cadillac having joined as the 11th team.
- Lando Norris (McLaren) is the reigning 2025 World Champion.
- Points are awarded to the top 10 finishers: 25-18-15-12-10-8-6-4-2-1.
- Starting in 2026, Apple TV is the exclusive Formula 1 broadcaster in the United States.
- The 2026 regulations are the biggest rule change since 2022, introducing active aerodynamics (movable front and rear wings) that replace DRS.
- Pirelli is the sole tyre supplier; teams must use at least two different dry tyre compounds per race.
- Six 2026 events are sprint weekends, with a short Saturday race that awards extra championship points.
